schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.

person.wealth_source: Oracle Corp. co-founder; 41% ownership stake; also significant Tesla holdings and Lawrence Investments family office

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 profile ~$227B; Wikipedia citing early-2026 Forbes ~$201B; CEO Today Magazine ~$258B. Peaked Sept 2025 at ~$393B before Oracle stock correction. The ~25% spread across 'Forbes 2026' sources is itself evidence that single-point net-worth denominators are unstable.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: Concentrated in Oracle (public, but 41% stake — divesting would move the stock). Tesla holdings are liquid. Lawrence Investments family office assets are private. Apply 25% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now' calculations; 70%+ for lifetime 'expected giving' denominator.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) — among 247 of 256 living pledgers who have not fulfilled
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: Many years ago, I put virtually all of my assets into a trust with the intent of giving away at least 95% of my wealth to charitable causes. I have already given hundreds of millions of dollars to medical research and education, and I will give billions more over time.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Wealth at signing ~$12B (Forbes 2010 estimate). 95% target in 2010 dollars = ~$11.4B. Current net worth is ~$227B; 95% target is now ~$216B. Gap between documented giving-out and pledge target: ~$215B.

pledges_and_announcements.quote_paraphrased_by_ip: I believed and continue to believe that practicing philanthropy by giving to nonprofit organizations that pursue public good is important. But there are additional ways that I would like to invest my time and resources in giving back to the world we share.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Inside Philanthropy describes this as Ellison 'amending' his 2010 Giving Pledge to include for-profit ventures through Ellison Institute of Technology. Standard Forbes/Chronicle/IPS methodology does not count for-profit investment as philanthropy.

pledges_and_announcements.recipient: Ellison Institute of Technology — Oxford campus
pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: Ellison Institute explicitly described by NYT as for-profit ventures alongside research. Classification under Forbes/IPS methodology: NOT counted as charitable giving until disbursed to a 501(c)(3) or foreign equivalent. Flag for reclassification when disbursement evidence appears.
Same for-profit hybrid concern as ellison_eit_oxford_1p3b
Fortune explicitly: 'not a charitable donation in the classic sense.' Included here for context on where Ellison's capital is being deployed; NOT counted as philanthropy.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Minimum floor from press sources; FEC API direct-pull not yet run. Includes $30M+ to Opportunity Matters Fund (pro-Tim Scott super-PAC) plus $1M to pro-Lindsey Graham PAC July 2025. Dollar figure will be tightened when Phase 1.3 FEC integration runs.

foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Grants out in 2024 ($76.4M) vs assets ($116.9M) = 65% — well above the 5% legal minimum; no LOW_PAYOUT flag.

foundations_terminated.name: Ellison Medical Foundation
foundations_terminated.status_note: Closed in 2013. At termination, Ellison stated he would continue medical research giving through personal vehicles. Successor 990-PF not identified in our data; pre-2013 grant-out history not yet retrieved.
foundations_terminated.todo: Pull archived 990-PFs 1997-2013 via IRS SOI or ProPublica archive to quantify actual grants_out before termination.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only — DAFs are donor-opaque by design. Assume unknown lower bound; see hidden_upper calculation.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Lawrence Investments is Ellison's family office / personal holding company — explicitly NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective.

for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.name: Ellison Institute of Technology (EIT)
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.year_established: ~2016 (as Lawrence Ellison Institute for Transformative Medicine at USC); reconstituted as standalone EIT more recently
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.methodology_note: NYT Aug 2025 describes EIT as a pivot to for-profit ventures plus research. Under Forbes/Chronicle/IPS methodology, for-profit impact investments do not count as charitable giving. EIT's pledges are NOT counted in observable giving until and unless disbursed to a 501(c)(3) or foreign charitable equivalent.

detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. Absence of public reporting so far; structure unknown.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: Many years ago, I put virtually all of my assets into a trust with the intent of giving away at least 95% of my wealth to charitable causes.
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: Ellison's 2010 pledge letter discloses the existence of a trust holding 'virtually all' his assets. Specific structure (revocable, grantor, CLT, CRT, dynasty, etc.) and jurisdiction not publicly disclosed. If it's a charitable lead/remainder trust, future giving is committed; if it's a dynasty or GRAT, it's primarily a wealth-transfer vehicle with uncertain charitable outcome. CRITICAL uncertainty.

red_flags.evidence: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) lists Ellison among 247 of 256 living pledgers who have not fulfilled. Observable giving ~$467M vs. pledge target ~$216B at current net worth.
$31M+ political giving 2021-2025 vs. ~$75M/yr foundation giving. Political giving to partisan super-PACs displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33/$1 per NBER W26616.

Ellison's 2025 Giving Pledge 'amendment' redirects resources to Ellison Institute of Technology, classified by NYT Aug 2025 as a for-profit hybrid. IPS explicitly excludes intermediary and for-profit vehicles from generosity measures.
Net worth grew from ~$12B at 2010 pledge signing to $393B peak in Sept 2025 (~33x). Annual LEF giving grew from $27.7M (2019) to $76.4M (2024) (~3x). Pledge fulfillment gap widened, not narrowed.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (LEF 2024 payout 65% of assets, well above 5% minimum)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified; not confirmed absent)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(LEF 990-PF grants_out 2019-2024) + USC 2016 direct_gift (NOT YET deduped against LEF 2016 990-PF). Excludes: transfers INTO EMF; EIT Oxford pledges (for-profit hybrid); political giving; Paramount investment.
observable_giving_todo: Pull LEF 990-PF years 1997-2018 to see if USC $200M 2016 gift is already captured in LEF grants_out (in which case subtract from direct_gifts to avoid double-count).
Pull Ellison Medical Foundation archived 990-PFs 1997-2013 — add grants_out for years active.
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Ellison personal CIK — check for stock gifts to LEF, USC, or other 501(c)(3).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.25 × $227.6B = $56.9B; tenure cap saturated (25 years). Expected = $5.69B. This is a STRICT denominator matching the formal 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Apply: 0.2% of liquid_wealth as cohort-median hidden anonymous giving upper bound. 0.002 × $227.6B × 0.25 liquidity = $114M; but Ellison is explicit in his pledge letter that he historically preferred privacy, so widen upper bound to 1% of observable giving × 100x cohort amplifier = $467M × 1.0 = $467M. Settling on $455M as a conservative upper.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Ellison's disclosed trust could eventually disburse to charity per his pledge (upper bound: most of $216B 95% target) OR primarily to heirs (dynasty-like outcome: ~0). Published lists should NOT credit undisbursed trust assets toward observable. We set an uncertainty envelope of ~$5B — representing what a 10-year disbursement schedule from the trust's charitable portion might plausibly deploy — to make the confidence interval visible, but we DO NOT include this in tier calculation. Flag: this coefficient needs justification before publication.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Ellison fails this test only because the trust_outcome_uncertainty is large — he has publicly stated that virtually all his wealth is in a trust with charitable intent. Without that disclosed trust, he would be Tier A Verified Low. WITH the trust, he's Tier B 'Probably Low' — publicly committed to giving but has not yet done so, and the trust's ultimate disposition is private.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Ellison as the canonical example of 'signed the Giving Pledge, hasn't followed through yet.' Observable giving ~$467M is 0.2% of current net worth and 8% of the 10% formal norm. The trust could still make good; as of 2026-04, it has not.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity with current pipeline. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.
